purpose: Define when Cursor runtime evidence may be promoted from projection smoke to native live-turn evidence.

officialEvidence.claim: Cursor CLI supports agent interaction from the terminal and can run interactive or print automation flows.
Cursor CLI supports rules, MCP, AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, and non-interactive print mode.
Cursor Agent CLI documents -p/--print and --output-format json or stream-json.
Cursor Agent CLI emits a single JSON object for --output-format json on successful --print runs, and failures exit non-zero without well-formed JSON.
Cursor Agent CLI installation is documented for macOS, Linux, and Windows WSL, and verifies with cursor-agent --version.

nativeHarnessCandidates.hostBoundary: Windows host invoking the official Windows WSL Cursor Agent CLI installation path.

passCriteria: A Cursor-native agent CLI is present or explicitly configured.
The CLI help output proves non-interactive print mode and structured output support.
The live prompt exits 0 and returns parseable JSON.
The JSON proves Warden -> Conductor -> orchestrationTaskBoardPacket -> workerTaskPackets.
The command runs without modifying files.
blockedCriteria: Only the Cursor IDE launcher is present.
WSL is present but cursor-agent is not installed or not on the WSL PATH.
The local command accepts agent arguments but does not expose -p/--print and --output-format.
The local command opens UI, emits only launcher help, or returns no parseable agent JSON.
Authentication is missing or the CLI requests interactive login.
The run would require unsafe file writes, approval bypass, or unbounded UI automation.
unsupportedWithReason: Projection smoke can validate .cursor files, rules, hooks, and mirrors, but release-grade Cursor live evidence requires a native agent CLI that can run a read-only non-interactive prompt and return parseable JSON.
